overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Score of 0 due to task incompletion within the 10-minute time limit (615 seconds). Your technique quality is excellent — this is purely a time management issue preventing you from demonstrating your true capability.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Expert-level technique held back by incomplete task execution

rubric_insights.observation: Task remained incomplete at 10:15 time limit. Only partial transfers completed despite excellent technique quality throughout visible portions.
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Complete score loss (300 points) due to incompletion penalty. With your technique quality, a completed task would likely score 200+ points.

rubric_insights.observation: 615 seconds used without task completion. Expert benchmark for full 6-object sequence is 45-90 seconds.
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Time penalty would be minimal if task completed — the incompletion penalty is the primary score killer.

technique_coaching.observation: In frames 1-18, technique quality is consistently excellent with smooth transfers and precise placements. However, the task appears to stall or restart multiple times rather than progressing through all 6 required transfers.
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Task 1 requires completing 6 objects transferred in sequence (3 left-to-right, 3 right-to-left). Incomplete execution negates all technical skill demonstrated.
technique_coaching.correction: Focus on continuous progression through the full sequence. Once an object is placed, immediately move to the next pickup without pause or hesitation.

drill.name: Full Sequence Sprint
drill.description: Complete all 6 transfers in one continuous motion. Set a timer for 2 minutes maximum. If you don't finish, restart immediately. Focus on flow, not perfection.
drill.success_criteria: Complete all 6 transfers within 2 minutes with no more than 1 drop per attempt

technique_coaching.observation: Individual transfer technique in frames 4-7 and 10-13 shows excellent control, but there appear to be extended pauses between transfers that consume valuable time.
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: With your technical skill level, you should complete Task 1 in under 90 seconds. Extended pauses suggest overthinking or loss of task focus.
technique_coaching.correction: Maintain continuous motion. As soon as one object is seated on its peg, your eyes should already be tracking to the next pickup location.

drill.name: Metronome Pacing
drill.description: Set a metronome to 60 BPM. Complete one full pickup-transfer-place cycle every 2 beats (2 seconds). Practice until this rhythm feels natural.
drill.success_criteria: Maintain 2-second cycle time for 10 consecutive transfers without drops

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Midair transfers in frames 6-7 and 12-13 show exceptional instrument coordination with no fumbling or object instability
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Clean midair transfers are the hallmark of advanced peg transfer skill. Your technique here is at expert level — many intermediate trainees struggle with drops during handoffs.
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your transfer technique is genuinely excellent. The scoring system cannot capture this quality when the task remains incomplete, but your motor skills are clearly advanced.

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Object placement precision in frames 10-11 shows perfect depth perception and peg alignment
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Precise placement without multiple positioning attempts saves 2-3 seconds per object. This efficiency multiplied across 6 objects creates significant time savings.
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your placement accuracy is spot-on. This precision will serve you well once you focus on completing the full sequence.

practice_plan.session_focus: Task completion within time limits — your technique is ready, execution strategy needs refinement

warmup_drill.name: Single Transfer Speed
warmup_drill.description: Pick up one object, transfer between hands, place on target peg. Repeat 10 times, timing each cycle.

primary_drill.name: Full Sequence Sprint
primary_drill.description: Complete all 6 transfers continuously. Set 90-second target initially, reduce by 10s each successful completion.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Complete full sequence in under 90 seconds with ≤1 drop

integration_drill.name: Pressure Practice
integration_drill.description: Complete 3 full sequences back-to-back with 30-second rest between. Simulate test conditions with time pressure.
integration_drill.success_criteria: All 3 sequences completed within individual 90-second limits

practice_plan.cooldown: Review: Did I maintain continuous motion? Where did I pause unnecessarily? What was my average completion time?
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 1-2 focused sessions to achieve consistent task completion, 3-4 sessions to reach 200+ FLS scores

progress_context.vs_baseline: Early sessions showed 500+ scores with 88-90s completion times. Recent attempts show incomplete execution despite maintained technique quality.
progress_context.vs_peak: Peak performance was 556 FLS (42s completion). Current 0 FLS represents execution failure, not skill regression.
progress_context.milestone_note: Next target: Complete any full sequence within 2 minutes → score ≥120 FLS. Then optimize to sub-90s → score ≥210 FLS.
progress_context.plateau_risk: High risk of frustration-induced technique degradation. Your motor skills are excellent — focus purely on execution completion.

coaching_limitations: Cannot determine exact point of task breakdown from frame sampling. Unclear whether this was equipment malfunction, distraction, or strategic confusion about task requirements.
disclaimer: AI coaching supplements but does not replace hands-on instruction from a qualified surgical educator. Discuss any technique changes with your program's skills lab proctor.
